enonce.texte:
Exercise 1: Match the disease with its symptom

Draw a line to match each disease with the correct symptom.

questions.texte: Match the disease with the correct symptom.

enonce.texte:
Exercise 2: Complete the sentences with the correct body part

Choose the correct word: head, stomach, throat, arm, leg.
images: A simple diagram of a human body with parts labeled.

questions.texte: I have a pain in my _______. I think it is because I ate too much.
She has a sore _______. She cannot speak well.
He hit his _______ on the door. It hurts.
My _______ hurts because I fell down.

enonce.texte:
Exercise 3: Complete the sentences with the correct past form of 'to be'

Use was or were to complete the sentences.

questions.texte: I _______ sick yesterday.
They _______ at the hospital last week.
She _______ very tired after the exam.
We _______ happy to see the doctor.
He _______ in bed all day.

enonce.texte:
Exercise 4: Complete the sentences with the correct past form of 'to have'

Use had to complete the sentences.

questions.texte: I _______ a fever last night.
She _______ a headache yesterday.
They _______ a cold last week.
We _______ malaria when we were young.
He _______ a stomachache after eating.

enonce.texte:
Exercise 5: Complete the sentences with the correct past form of 'to do'

Use did to complete the sentences.

questions.texte: I _______ my homework before I went to the doctor.
She _______ not take the medicine yesterday.
They _______ all their chores before they got sick.
We _______ not go to school because we were ill.
He _______ what the doctor told him.

intitule: Reading Comprehension: A Visit to the Doctor

enonce.texte:
Exercise 6: Read the dialogue and answer the questions

Doctor: Hello, Jean. What is wrong with you?

Jean: Good morning, Doctor. I have a headache and a fever.

Doctor: Do you have a cough?

Jean: Yes, I have a bad cough too.

Doctor: Let me check your temperature. You have a high fever. You have malaria.

Jean: What should I do, Doctor?

Doctor: You need to take this medicine. You should rest and drink a lot of water.

Jean: Thank you, Doctor.
images: A doctor talking to a patient.

questions.texte: What is wrong with Jean?
questions.reponse: He has a headache, a fever, and a cough.

questions.texte: What did the doctor find after checking Jean's temperature?
questions.reponse: The doctor found that Jean has a high fever.

questions.texte: What sickness does Jean have?
What did the doctor tell Jean to do?
questions.reponse: The doctor told Jean to take medicine, rest, and drink a lot of water.

intitule: Asking and Giving Information about Illnesses

enonce.texte:
Exercise 7: Complete the dialogue

Complete the conversation between a doctor and a patient using the words in the box.

Words: have, wrong, fever, medicine, rest
images: A doctor and a patient talking.

questions.texte: Doctor: Good morning! What is _______ with you?
Patient: I _______ a headache and a stomachache.
Doctor: Do you have a _______?
Patient: Yes, I think so.
Doctor: Take this _______ and get some _______.

enonce.texte:
Exercise 8: Write about a time you were sick

Write a short paragraph (4-5 sentences) describing a time when you were sick. Use the past tense forms of 'to be', 'to have', and 'to do'.

Use these questions to help you:

  • What was wrong with you?
  • What symptoms did you have?
  • What did you do?
  • Who helped you?
  • What did the doctor say?
images: A student writing in a notebook.

questions.texte: Write a paragraph about a time you were sick.
questions.reponse: Example answer: Last month, I was sick. I had a fever and a headache. I also had a cough. I did not go to school because I was very tired. My mother took me to the hospital. The doctor said I had malaria. He gave me medicine and told me to rest. I stayed in bed for three days. I felt better after taking the medicine.

enonce.texte:
Exercise 9: Role-Play a Doctor-Patient Conversation

Work in pairs. One student is the doctor and the other is the patient. The patient describes their symptoms and the doctor gives advice. Use the expressions below.

Useful expressions:

  • What is wrong with you?
  • I have a headache / stomachache / cough / fever.
  • When did it start?
  • You need to take this medicine.
  • You should rest.
  • You should drink water.
  • Thank you, Doctor.
images: A doctor and a patient talking.

questions.texte: Write your dialogue here.
questions.reponse:
Example dialogue:

Doctor: Good morning! What is wrong with you?

Patient: Good morning, Doctor. I have a terrible headache and a sore throat.

Doctor: Do you have a fever too?

Patient: Yes, I think I have a fever.

Doctor: Let me check your temperature. Yes, you have a fever. When did it start?

Patient: It started yesterday evening.

Doctor: You have a bad cold. You need to take this medicine. You should also rest and drink a lot of water.

Patient: Thank you, Doctor. I will do that.

Doctor: Get well soon!

enonce.texte:
Exercise 10: Write a letter giving advice

Imagine your friend is sick. Write a short letter to your friend giving advice on what to do to get better.

Use these expressions:

  • You should...
  • You need to...
  • You shouldn't...
  • You must...
  • Take this medicine...
  • Rest and drink water...

questions.texte: Write a letter to your sick friend.
questions.reponse:
Example answer:

Dear Marie,

I heard that you are sick. I am sorry to hear that. You should go to the hospital and see a doctor. You must take the medicine the doctor gives you. You shouldn't go to school until you feel better. You need to rest in bed and drink a lot of water. You should also eat healthy food. Please take care of yourself. I hope you get better soon.

Your friend,
Jean
